rows.description: Soluta Varia
rows.notes: Top-center title is clear: reads 'Soluta Varia' in Latin script. No amounts on this line and ruling lines do not intersect the title.

rows.description: Account Dinner
rows.notes: Clear left-hand heading immediately under title. No monetary figures on the same line.

rows.description: Pr. Tregoze's bill for Proc. of Ed:wick, Walboji, & Collison
rows.notes: Description mostly legible though names are abbreviated; pounds/shillings/pence alignment is clear (1-4-6). Handwriting of surnames uncertain but context (a bill) supports reading.

rows.description: Pr. Hillyard ye Glazier's bill
rows.notes: Description reads 'Hillyard ye Glazier's bill' fairly clearly. Numeric columns well aligned and read as 5 pounds, 15 shillings, 0 pence.

rows.description: Pr. Wm. Taggin for smiths
rows.notes: The name is uncertain ('Taggin' is best visual match). The pounds column clearly shows '20', shillings and pence are zeros. Moderate uncertainty on exact spelling of the name.

rows.description: Pr. Cook for fuel for the May
rows.notes: Description 'for fuel for the May' is legible; amount columns read 11-10-0 with good alignment.

rows.description: Pr. for being? Brawns at May
rows.notes: Description is faded and partially obscured; appears to refer to 'being brawns' or similar. Amount reads 0-1-0 but handwriting and column alignment are faint here.

rows.description: Pr. Books for bringing Ladame ? book
rows.notes: Description uncertain ('Ladame' uncertain); monetary columns read 0 pounds, 2 shillings, 6 pence. Slight ink smudge near shillings but within column.

rows.description: Pr. for bringing of Capony
rows.notes: Description 'bringing of Capony' plausible. Pence column shows '2' but there is a trailing loop that could indicate 6d or a halfpenny mark; interpreted here as 2 pence with a decorative loop read as extra flourish — low confidence.

rows.description: Pr. for a buttery book
rows.notes: Reads 'for a buttery book' clearly. Amount columns aligned to 0-10-6; pence digit legible as 6.

rows.description: Pr. for Gunni for surveying, or care & trouble of affairs of palane
rows.notes: Description partly faded but 'surveying' and 'care & trouble' visible. Amounts read 3-13-6 though '13' shillings requires careful column alignment; acceptable but moderate uncertainty.

rows.description: Pr. G. Gregor's bill
rows.notes: Surname appears as 'G. Gregor' (first initial and surname). Amount columns clearly read 0-16-0.

rows.description: Pr. Bleyt(?) tax for two year
rows.notes: Handwriting of item label is unclear ('Bleyt' is a guess). Amount columns read 0-13-0. Moderate uncertainty on description.

rows.description: Pr. for a common prayer book for ye Chappell
rows.notes: Phrase 'common prayer book for ye Chappell' is legible. Amount appears as 0-10-7 with clear digits.

rows.description: Pr. for cook for new laundry
rows.notes: Reading of 'cook for new laundry' uncertain but plausible; amount reads 11-10-0. Slight ambiguity on description wording.

rows.description: Pr. for fuel for Lady Day
rows.notes: Line reads 'for fuel for lady day' though the first word before 'fuel' is faint. Amount columns seem to show 11-9-7 but pence digit is slightly smudged (7 chosen by alignment).

rows.description: Pr. Glauser's Bill
rows.notes: Surname appears as 'Glauser' or 'Glazier' variant; amount 1-9-0 aligns well. Moderate confidence on exact name spelling.

rows.description: Pr. Mr. Gaignor
rows.notes: Name reads 'Mr. Gaignor' (uncertain spelling). Amount 9-18-0 is clear in numeric columns, though the 18 shillings is close to the 20 boundary and required careful column alignment.

rows.description: Pr. for 2 year's Candles for ye Chappell
rows.notes: The phrase '2 year's Candles for ye Chappell' is legible. Amount reads 1-0-0 with clear alignment.

rows.description: Pr. for sweeping court at Little Rue
rows.notes: Description 'sweeping court at Little Rue' (or 'Little Row') readable; amount 0-3-0 clear.

rows.description: Pr. for a Bell rope
rows.notes: Entry 'for a Bell rope' clear. Amount reads 0-17-0 clearly aligned in columns.

rows.description: Pr. for Linnen for ye Chairs & table
rows.notes: Text references 'Linnen for ye Chappell table' (wording approximated). Numeric 0-2-0 is clear.

rows.description: Pr. for making of napkins
rows.notes: Phrase 'making of napkins' plausible though the initial abbreviation is faint. Amount appears as 11-10-0 but the leading '11' is faint and could be 1 or 11; chosen 11 by alignment with neighboring rows.

rows.description: Pr. Cook for feast for Midsummer
rows.notes: Description reads 'cook for feast for midsummer' (Midsummer spelled variably). Amount 1-2-6 visible though slightly faded in pence digit.

rows.description: Pr. for a thirty book
rows.notes: Text appears to be 'for a thirty book' (meaning uncertain). Amount 0-10-0 is clear in columns.

rows.description: Pr. Glauser's Bill (another)
rows.notes: A second 'Glazier/Glauser' bill appears; handwriting is cramped. Amount reads 8-15-6 but pence is smudged and the '8' pounds is not as clear as neighboring numbers.

rows.description: Pr. for candles for ye Chappell misc:
rows.notes: Entry clearly refers to 'candles for ye Chappell'. Amount columns read 13-16-0 with good alignment.

rows.description: Pr. for candy for ye Hall, buttery, other, kitchen, gate
rows.notes: Text lists places where candy was used; the list is faint at the end but legible. Amount 1-0-0 seen though the '1' is small.

rows.description: Pr. Mr. Gaignor
rows.notes: Appears to be another line for 'Mr. Gaignor' or similar name. Amount reads 7-4-9 but the pence 9 is faint and the surname uncertain.

rows.description: Pr. Mr. Palmer's bill
rows.notes: Surname read as 'Palmer' or 'Palers' — marginal uncertainty. Amount 2-7-0 aligns clearly with shilling column.

rows.description: Pr. J. Smithe's bill
rows.notes: Name is faint but looks like 'Smithe' or 'Smiths'. Amount 11-10-0 appears; the first digit could be 1 or 11 but aligns with an '11' pattern used elsewhere.

rows.description: Pr. ye cook for fowls for nic:
rows.notes: Description truncated 'fowls for nic:' unclear; likely a small household expense. Amount 0-10-0 is legible but description uncertain.

rows.description: Pr. Mr. Clark's bill for Pen, ink, paper, bottle books, &c
rows.notes: List of stationery items is readable. Amount 0-2-6 clear; the ampersand and trailing abbreviation '&c' are visible.

rows.description: Pr. Mr. Brooks for bringing Ladame (?) book
rows.notes: Name 'Brooks' readable; remainder of description faint. Amount 0-8-0 is clear but description uncertain (likely similar to an earlier 'bringing' entry).

rows.description: Backgate Blayt? Tax
rows.notes: Short entry 'Backgate' with a following word that looks like 'Blayt' or 'Bleyt'; amount 0-8-0 visible. Ink shadowing and abbreviated handwriting reduce confidence.

rows.description: Boyce? Tax
rows.notes: Very short entry; possibly 'Boyce Tax' or similar. Amount 0-8-0 repeated. Low confidence due to extremely faint script and shadow on the page.

rows.description: Pr. Mr. Ireland full ? cont. his eight pounds agre, on his giving a plate of two pounds fiftee shillings & half pence
rows.notes: Long marginal note at bottom-left is densely written and partially cut off; appears to record an agreement of eight pounds. The numeric 8-0-0 in the pounds column is clear, but the surrounding text has many abbreviations and ambiguous words.

rows.description: Sum
rows.notes: Final total in bottom-right reads '150. 18. 9d' (150 pounds, 18 shillings, 9 pence). Alignment is typical for final sum and digits are clearly written.
